The current bibliography covers technical debt (Sculley et al.), distribution shift/OOD generalization (Koh et al.), and uncertainty quantification (Wang et al.). However, it lacks a foundational reference specifically addressing model fragility under common, naturally occurring corruptions and perturbations (e.g., noise, blur, weather, compression), which is a major real-world deployment pitfall. Adding Hendrycks & Dietterich's seminal work on corruption robustness will comprehensively cover Categories 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, and 8, while bridging the gap between academic clean-accuracy benchmarks and physical-world reliability. This is the most critical missing citation to round out the paper's empirical and methodological grounding.
